word: H3920 - לָכַד - lâkad - law-kad' - [idiom] at all

sections.paragraph:
Original: לכד

Transliteration: lâkad

Phonetic: law-kad'

BDB Definition:

  1. to capture, take, seize
    1. (Qal)
      1. to capture, seize
      2. to capture (of men) (figuratively)
      3. to take (by lot)
    2. (Niphal)
      1. to be captured
      2. to be caught (of men in trap, snare) (figuratively)
    3. (Hithpael) to grasp each other

Origin: a primitive root

TWOT entry: 1115

Part(s) of speech: Verb
